session_id: ANL-2026-04-25-l2规则使命宣言业务伤疤融入评判
topic: 评判 L2 规则 [MISSION_STATEMENT] 与 [BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS] 应如何融入 Fabric-v2
summary: MISSION_STATEMENT should be added as a first-class L2 Markdown section fetched through the existing L2 required path-triggered rule flow. BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS should also be a structured L2 section, but requires a separate anchor governance loop: parse @fabric-anchor comments, validate chunk-anchor resolution, warn during pre-commit when anchors disappear, and surface stale/archive diagnostics through doctor. Both blocks should stay out of RuleDescription so plan_context remains a neutral lightweight index.

key_conclusions.point: MISSION_STATEMENT fits the existing L2 path-required mechanism.
The rule section protocol must be expanded beyond MANDATORY_INJECTION and CONTEXT_INFO.
BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS needs anchor lifecycle governance, not just Markdown injection.
Dynamic pruning should initially mean stale diagnostics and auditable archival, not silent removal.

recommendations.action: Extend structured rule sections with MISSION_STATEMENT and BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS
recommendations.rationale: The current section fetch API is the right delivery point, but the whitelist currently excludes the proposed blocks.

steps.description: Add the two names to RULE_SECTION_NAMES and update RuleSectionName consumers.
steps.verification: Parser/service tests can request and receive both sections.

steps.description: Update MCP tool schema section enums.
steps.verification: Tool registration tests expose the new enum values.

steps.description: Document section ordering and missing-section diagnostics.
steps.verification: Docs describe the recommended fetch order and no-full-content fallback behavior.

recommendations.action: Define the L2 Markdown contract for mission statements and business chunks
recommendations.rationale: The value of both sections depends on predictable structure that agents can parse and humans can review.

steps.description: Add documentation/template examples for MISSION_STATEMENT fields.
steps.verification: Docs list Sovereignty, Physical Boundary, Long-term Contract, and Engineering Value.

steps.description: Add documentation/template examples for BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS fields.
steps.verification: Docs list ID, Anchor, Intent, Scars, and Constraint.

steps.description: Add test fixtures for non-trivial business chunk parsing.
steps.verification: Fixtures preserve structured chunk text without losing repeated sections.

recommendations.rationale: The business memory requirement explicitly depends on comments remaining in code even when code moves.

steps.description: Implement a scanner for @fabric-anchor IDs in source files.
steps.verification: Tests resolve anchor ID to file and line.

steps.description: Validate BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS anchors against matched L2 scope files.
steps.verification: Diagnostics report missing, duplicated, and stale anchors.

steps.description: Wire anchor warnings into pre-commit and detailed reporting into doctor.
steps.verification: Pre-commit test warns when a staged file removes a referenced anchor.

recommendations.action: Implement conservative chunk merge and pruning semantics
recommendations.rationale: Business scars are safety data; automatic deletion should be auditable and reversible.

steps.description: Treat same chunk ID as replace/upsert.
steps.target: rule chunk parser or future registry update helper
steps.verification: Duplicate ID fixtures produce deterministic latest-chunk behavior or diagnostics.
Doctor reports stale business chunks without modifying source.

steps.description: Add explicit archival path for stale chunks.
steps.verification: Archive operation is explicit and ledger/audit visible.

open_questions: Should missing @fabric-anchor be a warning by default and error under strict config?
Should anchor comments support block comments and language-specific forms beyond //?
Should archived stale chunks live under .fabric/archive/business-logic-chunks.jsonl or in an audit event stream?

intent_coverage.notes: Use L2 required selection plus first section ordering.
Section carries memory, anchor governance carries lifecycle.
Initial implementation should use deterministic upsert and auditable stale archive.

findings_coverage.finding: L2 required path trigger already exists
Section whitelist lacks the new names
Business chunks require anchor lifecycle checks
findings_coverage.reason: Commit warning cannot be achieved by section injection alone.

findings_coverage.finding: Dynamic pruning should not silently delete scars
findings_coverage.reason: Historical business memory must remain auditable.

findings_coverage.finding: Tooling manifest is precedent but not target registry
findings_coverage.reason: It confirms script-boundary knowledge is useful, but intentionally separate from agents.meta.

decision_trail.decision: Use section protocol for both proposed L2 blocks.
decision_trail.context: Existing rule section API already serves structured Markdown sections after path context is known.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: RuleDescription should remain lightweight and neutral; agents.meta should not store business正文.
decision_trail.reason: Lowest-friction integration with existing L2 required selection.
decision_trail.impact: Implementation scope starts in rule-sections service/tool/tests.

decision_trail.decision: Add separate anchor governance for BUSINESS_LOGIC_CHUNKS.
decision_trail.context: User requires deletion of @fabric-anchor comments to trigger commit warning.
decision_trail.rejected_reasons: Prompt-only behavior is not enforceable; doctor-only feedback is too late for commit-time warning.
decision_trail.reason: Matches existing Fabric governance pattern around human-lint and doctor.
decision_trail.impact: Requires new anchor parser and diagnostics.
